sections.title: The Most Central Continent
points: Africa is the only continent crossed by the Equator, Tropic of Cancer, Tropic of Capricorn, and the Prime Meridian.
Because of this, Africa is located in all four hemispheres: Northern, Southern, Eastern, and Western.
The Equator and Prime Meridian meet in the Gulf of Guinea off the coast of West Africa.

sections.title: African Countries on the Prime Meridian (0°)
points: The Prime Meridian crosses four main countries in Africa:

sections.title: African Countries on the Equator (0°)
points: Central/West: DRC, Congo, Gabon, Sao Tome and Principe.

sections.title: Africa's Exact Grid Location
points: Because 75% of Africa lies between the two Tropics, it is the hottest continent.

sections.text: PLE Fact: The only African city famously crossed by the Prime Meridian is Accra, the capital of Ghana.

cards.fact: Countries in Africa that the 0° latitude line passes through.
Another name for the Prime Meridian because it passes through Greenwich in London.
